name: Inappropriate Expression
strauss_status: EXPLICITLY NAMED AS A POSSIBLE DEVICE

source.primary_work: Persecution and the Art of Writing
source.primary_essay: The Literary Character of the Guide for the Perplexed
source.source_mechanism: A seemingly ill-fitting expression may function as a stumbling block that directs attention beneath the surface.
source.documentary_limit: Strauss notes that it can also be dismissed as merely inappropriate; therefore it does not compel inquiry as strongly as a contradiction.

mechanism: A bounded expression conflicts with an independently documented lexical, grammatical, semantic, register, pragmatic, technical, or contextual fit baseline. The mismatch materially interrupts the surface reading and may function as a stumbling block that authorizes a bounded question about why this expression occurs here. The anomaly remains weaker evidence than a contradiction and may prove to be merely inappropriate.
investigative_requirement: Preserve the exact expression; establish the fit baseline before calling it inappropriate; document the mismatch under historically and linguistically appropriate standards; verify source language, grammar, translation, speaker, genre, technical vocabulary, and witnesses; formulate only a bounded textual question; test every ordinary repair and explanation; and require independent corroboration before treating the expression as more than a possible signal.
distinctions: An unusual, inelegant, or disliked expression is not automatically inappropriate. A documented fit baseline is required.
The expression may be merely inappropriate. LC-021 therefore authorizes weaker evidentiary force than an established contradiction.
The authorized response is a bounded textual question, not an inferred hidden answer.
LC-006 concerns multiple lexical meanings. LC-021 concerns a documented mismatch between an expression and its expected local or technical fit.
LC-008 concerns concealment in an unobtrusive common word. LC-021 requires the expression to operate as a noticeable stumbling block.
LC-020 requires a finite cue-to-target discovery path. LC-021 may supply a possible cue, but it does not itself establish that path.
LC-022 concerns an awkward transition between subjects rather than the fit of a bounded expression.
The evaluator may corroborate an inappropriate-expression signal but may not infer hidden meaning, authorial intention, intended audience, concealment, or doctrinal truth.

trigger_conditions: A bounded expression is recoverable in a stable witness.
An independent and historically appropriate fit baseline is documented.
The expression conflicts with that baseline in a specific way.
The mismatch materially disrupts or complicates the surface reading.
Documentary support shows that the mismatch may function as a stumbling block.
A bounded textual question can be formulated without asserting a hidden answer.
minimum_evidence: Exact expression text and stable witness location.
Declared textual boundary of the expression.
Historically and linguistically appropriate support for the baseline.
Local sentence, paragraph, and argumentative context.
Architectonic context within chapter, section, and work.
Resolved speaker, narrator, quotation, objection, hypothetical, and reported-opinion attribution.
Source-language form, morphology, syntax, semantic range, and technical usage where available.
Translation, edition, punctuation, and textual-variant review.
Authorial usage, parallel-passage, and genre comparison.
A bounded attention-question record that does not assert an answer.
An auditable evidence path from witness through fit baseline and mismatch to the bounded question.
corroboration_indicators: The author uses the expected expression consistently elsewhere in comparable contexts.
The same anomalous expression recurs at another marked location.
A parallel passage repairs or clarifies the mismatch.
The expression converges with an independently documented contradiction or hint path.
The mismatch is preserved across authoritative witnesses and translations.
A source comparison shows controlled substitution, retention, or alteration.
Removing or replacing the expression materially changes the local interpretive difficulty.
The anomaly occurs at an architectonically marked location.
ordinary_alternatives: Textual corruption, copyist error, punctuation, or witness variation.
The fit baseline is mistaken, anachronistic, or too narrow.
The expression is merely inappropriate and has no further function.
disqualifying_conditions: No independent fit baseline is documented.
The mismatch depends only on taste, elegance, or modern usage.
The expression is appropriate in the source language or technical context.
Translation, grammar, idiom, genre, speaker, source quotation, or witness variation fully explains the expression.
The mismatch has no material effect on the surface reading.
No documentary basis shows that it functions as a stumbling block.
The bounded question already assumes a hidden answer.
The anomaly disappears after the correct textual boundary is restored.
The expression belongs to a different speaker or quoted source and that attribution explains it.
Another technique fully explains the feature and no distinct LC-021 operation remains.
authorized_response: Preserve the exact expression and complete context.
Create a separate fit-baseline record before classifying the mismatch.
Create a typed mismatch record and preserve uncertainty.
Create a bounded textual question asking why this expression occurs here.
Test source language, grammar, technical usage, speaker, genre, translation, witnesses, and ordinary repairs.
Compare authorial usage and parallel passages.
Search for independent corroboration before escalating the signal.
Evaluate LC-006, LC-008, LC-020, or LC-022 separately when independently triggered.
Preserve the possibility that the expression is merely inappropriate.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer significance from awkwardness alone.
Do not use aesthetic dislike as a fit baseline.
Do not infer a hidden meaning from the expression.
Do not treat LC-021 as equivalent in force to a contradiction.
Do not compel deep inquiry without independent corroboration.
Do not infer authorial intention, intended audience, concealment, or doctrinal truth.
Do not silently repair the authorial wording.
Do not collapse LC-021 into ambiguity, hint, common-word concealment, or clumsy transition.
operational.uncertainty_rule: When a documented mismatch exists but the fit baseline, ordinary usage, stumbling-block function, or alternative explanations remain disputed, preserve a candidate inappropriate-expression signal and the bounded question it raises. Preserve explicitly the possibility that the expression is merely inappropriate.
operational.termination_rule: Terminate as NOT_TRIGGERED when no historically grounded mismatch, no material disruption, or no supported stumbling-block function exists; as BLOCKED_MISSING_EVIDENCE when source, boundary, baseline, language, context, speaker, alternatives, question, or provenance is incomplete; as candidate when ordinary alternatives or corroboration remain unresolved; and as corroborated only when the expression's anomalous and attention-directing function survives full documentary testing. No outcome compels inquiry as strongly as a contradiction or establishes hidden meaning.

version_history.change_note: Initial executable projection of LC-021 with independent fit baselines, typed mismatch records, stumbling-block evidence, bounded questions, ordinary-repair testing, weak evidentiary force, and explicit preservation of the merely-inappropriate alternative.
